title: Mega Scizor Battle Guide
summary: The canonical [technician] priority abuser — [bullet-punch] becomes an effective 60 BP [steel] STAB, and post-[swords-dance] cleanup is devastating.
strengths: [swords-dance] turns it into an unstoppable sweeper
Nine resistances including a 4x Fairy resist
weaknesses: 4x Fire weakness — dies to any stray flamethrower
Speed 75 is outpaced by most Scarf users
Pre-Mega turn still uses normal Scizor stats

recommendedMoves.reason: Priority [steel] STAB boosted to 60 BP by [technician]
+2 Atk setup that makes cleanup guaranteed
Item removal utility for support disruption
Secures Mega turn and setup window

recommendedItems.reason: Mandatory Mega Stone with no alternative

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
Mega Scizor is the definitional [technician] priority sweeper. [bullet-punch]'s 40 BP is raised to 60 BP by the ability, then STABbed into an effective ~90 BP [steel] priority move — one of the strongest priority attacks in the metagame. After one [swords-dance], most frail threats are cleanly KO'd before they can move.

## Strengths
[bullet-punch] pressures Fairy, Ice, Rock, and Psychic attackers from priority range, so Mega Scizor rarely needs to outspeed anything. [bug]/[steel] typing grants nine resistances including a 4x Fairy resist — it walls threats like tapu-koko and togekiss for free. Base 140 Attack plus [swords-dance] pushes its KO ranges deep into bulky targets.

## Weaknesses & Answers
4x Fire is the headline weakness — heatran, charizard, and random flamethrower users will OHKO. A single will-o-wisp cripples the entire set, so Fire attackers must be switched out of. Speed 75 is slow for a sweeper, so the game plan leans heavily on priority rather than winning speed ties.

## Recommended Moveset
Lock in [bullet-punch]. [swords-dance] is the setup move — one boost is usually enough. [knock-off] punishes switch-ins by removing items; note that at 65 BP it falls outside [technician] range but still provides utility. [protect] is mandatory for the Mega turn and for setup windows in doubles.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[scizorite] is mandatory. [technician] activates post-Mega. Tera [steel] boosts STAB damage on [bullet-punch] and patches the Fire 4x weakness, while Tera [bug] unlocks maximum u-turn / [bug]-based damage on slower cores.

## Teammates & Synergy
Pair with a Water or Ground attacker to cover the Fire weakness, and use incineroar intimidate + fake-out to buy setup time. A tailwind setter offsets Mega Scizor's mediocre speed for when priority isn't enough.

## Play Patterns
Protect on the Mega turn, then find a window for [swords-dance]. After boosting, spam [bullet-punch] on frail threats and save Tera for an incoming Fire move. Preserve Mega Scizor late-game so its priority cleans up remaining attackers.
